title: 10 best herbal teas for dry hair
intro_desc: Herbal teas for dry hair are infusions made from various dried herbs, flowers, and seeds that are known to moisturize and nourish dry hair. These teas are rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that help to improve the health and appearance of dry hair. Some examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for dry hair include chamomile, calendula, horsetail, and nettle. Chamomile tea, for instance, contains anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe a dry, itchy scalp, while calendula tea is known for its ability to stimulate hair growth and prevent hair loss. Horsetail tea is rich in silica, which can strengthen hair and promote shine, while nettle tea is packed with vitamins and minerals that can improve hair elasticity and prevent breakage. By incorporating these herbal teas into their hair care routine, individuals with dry hair can experience softer, more manageable hair that is less prone to breakage and damage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with dry hair because it contains inflammation-reducing and moisture-retaining properties that can improve the condition of dry and damaged hair. The plant's anti-inflammatory compounds can help soothe a dry, itchy scalp, while its emollient properties can lock in moisture and protect the hair shaft from further damage. Additionally, yarrow tea is rich in vitamins and minerals, including vitamin E, which can help promote hair growth and prevent breakage. By drinking yarrow tea or using it as a rinse, individuals can nourish their hair and scalp, leaving them with softer, shinier, and more manageable hair.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: Yarrow tea contains several hydrating compounds that help to lock in moisture and alleviate dryness in hair, preventing brittleness and breakage. Active constituents include flavonoids, tannins, and saponins.

Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an irritated scalp and reduce inflammation, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing dryness-related irritation. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: Yarrow tea contains powerful antioxidants that help protect the hair from environmental damage and oxidative stress, which can lead to dryness and hair aging. The main active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Improved blood circulation: Yarrow tea helps improve blood flow to the scalp, promoting nutrient absorption and stimulating healthy hair growth, which can help reduce dryness and brittleness. The active constituents responsible for this property are volatile oils, such as azulene and chamazulene.

Balanced sebum production: Yarrow tea has a balancing effect on sebum production, reducing excess oiliness and promoting a healthy scalp environment, which helps prevent dryness and flakiness. The active constituents responsible for this property are sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nutrients and antioxidants, yarrow leaves promote hair growth and help moisturize dry hair.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, yarrow flowers soothe the scalp, reducing dryness and irritation.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, yarrow seeds nourish the hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing dryness.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried yarrow herb, simmering for 15-20 minutes.
After straining the mixture, let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
In a separate container, mix the cooled yarrow tea with 1 cup of warm water.
Before shampooing, thoroughly wet your hair and pour the yarrow tea mixture over it, massaging gently into the scalp.
Leave the mixture on for 10-15 minutes, then rinse it out and proceed with your usual hair care routine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with dry hair because it contains mucilage, a type of plant gelatin that can moisturize and soften hair. This gel-like substance coats the hair shaft, sealing in moisture and providing a protective barrier against damage from styling and environmental factors. Additionally, marshmallow tea is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to soothe a dry, itchy scalp and promote healthy hair growth. By drinking marshmallow tea or using it as a rinse, individuals with dry hair can experience increased hydration, reduced frizz, and improved overall hair health.
remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Marshmallow tea contains mucilage, a gel-like substance that creates a protective film on the hair, helping to lock in moisture and alleviate dryness.
Emollient property: The high mucilage content in marshmallow tea smoothes the hair cuticle, promoting shine and reducing breakage caused by dry, brittle hair.
Anti-inflammatory property: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in marshmallow tea help reduce scalp inflammation, enhancing hair growth and minimizing dry, flaky skin.
Humectant property: Marshmallow tea attracts and retains moisture from the environment, hydrating dry hair and preventing further dryness and damage.
Nutrient-rich: Rich in vitamins and minerals, marshmallow tea nourishes dry hair, promoting hair strength, elasticity, and overall hair health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nutrients, the leaves help to nourish and moisturize dry hair.
Flowers: Contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that promote hair growth and soothe dry, itchy scalp.
Seeds: High in fatty acids, seeds provide essential nourishment to dry hair, promoting shine and softness.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of dried nettle leaves, and 1 tablespoon of dried horsetail herb; bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove from heat, add 1 tablespoon of dried marshmallow root, and let the mixture steep for 20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herbs, then compost the solids.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Pour the tea over freshly washed hair, massaging it into the scalp and allowing it to sit for 10-15 minutes before rinsing thoroughly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tea helps with dry hair because it contains phytosterols and essential fatty acids that nourish and moisturize the hair follicles, enhancing hair shine and softness. Its high antioxidant content also helps to protect the hair from environmental damage, while its anti-inflammatory properties can soothe a dry, itchy scalp. Furthermore, burdock tea is rich in vitamins and minerals, including vitamin A, B-complex, and E, which promote hair growth and strength, leaving your hair looking healthy and vibrant.
remedy_properties: Nourishing properties: Burdock tea contains essential fatty acids and vitamins that nourish and moisturize dry hair, promoting shine and suppleness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The presence of tannins and essential oils in burdock tea helps reduce scalp inflammation, which can lead to dry hair and dandruff.
Blood circulation improvement: Burdock tea stimulates blood flow in the scalp, ensuring proper nutrient distribution and hydration for dry hair.
Detoxification properties: Rich in antioxidants, burdock tea helps detoxify the scalp and hair follicles, eliminating impurities that can cause dryness and brittleness.
Hormonal balance: Burdock tea may help regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to dry hair, as it contains plant-based phytoestrogens.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots are rich in fatty oils and phytosterols that can help moisturize and nourish dry hair.
Leaves: Burdock leaves contain significant amounts of silica and essential amino acids that can help strengthen and protect dry hair from damage.
Seeds: Burdock seeds are a great source of vitamin E and fatty acids that can help improve hair elasticity and prevent dry hair from breaking.
Buds: Burdock buds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe a dry, itchy scalp and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried burdock root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat, and let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Strain the tea to remove the burdock root, and discard the solid remains.
Pour the tea into a spray bottle, and apply it evenly onto clean, damp hair before styling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tea helps with dry hair because it contains powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can improve scalp health. The tea is made from the Artemisia absinthium plant, which is rich in vitamins and minerals that nourish the hair and scalp. Wormwood tea can help stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting hair growth and reducing hair fall. The tea's moisturizing properties can help hydrate and soften dry, brittle hair, leaving it looking shiny and healthy. Regular use of wormwood tea can help improve the overall health and appearance of dry, damaged hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood tea's anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to compounds like sesquiterpene lactones, can help reduce scalp inflammation, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth and reducing dryness.

Antimicrobial: Wormwood tea contains antimicrobial constituents, such as artemisinin and flavonoids, which can help maintain a clean and healthy scalp by inhibiting the growth of microorganisms that may contribute to dryness and dandruff.
Antioxidant: Wormwood tea is rich in antioxidants, including phenolic acids and flavonoids, which can help protect the hair and scalp from oxidative stress, promoting moisturized and healthy hair.

Circulation-enhancing: Wormwood tea's circulation-enhancing properties, due to compounds like camphor and 1,8-cineole, can help improve blood flow to the scalp, ensuring that essential nutrients reach the hair follicles, promoting hair growth and reducing dryness.
Hydration-promoting: Wormwood tea contains hydrating compounds, such as polysaccharides, that can help lock in moisture, providing dry hair with added hydration and reducing brittleness and breakage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nutrients and antioxidants, wormwood leaves help nourish and strengthen dry hair.
Flowers: Containing essential oils, wormwood flowers help moisturize and condition dry hair, promoting shine and softness.
Seeds: High in fatty acids, wormwood seeds help restore natural oils to dry hair, improving elasticity and reducing breakage.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried wormwood herb; let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Turn off the heat, cover the pot, and let the tea steep for an additional 30 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herbs, and let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Shampoo your hair as usual, then pour the wormwood tea over your hair, massaging it into your scalp.
Leave the tea in your hair for 10-15 minutes before rinsing it out with warm water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with dry hair because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and nourish the scalp. The tea is also rich in vitamin E and flavonoids, which can help to improve blood circulation in the scalp, promoting hair growth and reducing hair breakage. Furthermore, pot marigold tea has emollient properties that can help to lock in moisture, leaving hair feeling soft, smooth, and hydrated. Regular use of pot marigold tea can help to strengthen dry hair, making it more manageable and less prone to split ends.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: Pot marigold tea is rich in flavonoids and carotenoids, which help to moisturize and hydrate dry hair, preventing further damage and breakage.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of pot marigold tea, due to its high content of flavonoids and essential oils, can soothe an irritated scalp, reducing dryness and flakiness.
Antioxidant: Pot marigold tea contains powerful antioxidants like lutein and zeaxanthin, which protect the hair from environmental stressors and free radicals, keeping it healthy and hydrated.
Emollient: The emollient properties of pot marigold tea, derived from its essential oils and waxes, help to smooth the hair cuticle, sealing in moisture and adding shine to dry hair.

Circulation-boosting: Pot marigold tea can help to improve blood circulation in the scalp, promoting hair growth and ensuring that nutrients and moisture reach the hair follicles, preventing dryness and promoting overall hair health.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, pot marigold flowers help nourish and moisturize dry hair.
Leaves: High in vitamins and minerals, pot marigold leaves promote hair growth and help restore dry hair's natural shine.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids, pot marigold seeds help strengthen dry hair and reduce breakage.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried marigold flowers and place them in a tea infuser or loose leaf tea bag.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the marigold flowers.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or tea bag and let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature.
Gently pour the marigold tea over freshly washed hair, massaging it into the scalp and leaving it on for 10-15 minutes before rinsing with cool water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with dry hair because it is rich in antioxidants and nutrients that can improve the health of your hair. It contains high levels of vitamin C, E, and B, which can help to strengthen hair follicles and promote hair growth. White tea also has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce scalp inflammation and dandruff, which can contribute to dry hair. Additionally, white tea contains flavonoids and tannins that can help to protect your hair from environmental damage, such as UV rays and pollution, which can dry out your hair. Regularly drinking white tea or using it as a rinse can help to moisturize and nourish your hair, leaving it soft, shiny, and more manageable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce scalp inflammation, promoting a healthy scalp and reducing dryness. This is due to the presence of tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.

Antioxidant: White tea is rich in antioxidants that protect the hair from free radical damage, preventing dryness and breakage. The high concentration of EGCG (epigallocatechin gallate) and other catechins contribute to this property.

Moisturizing: White tea has moisturizing properties that help maintain the hair's natural moisture, reducing dryness and improving hair texture. The presence of amino acids, vitamins, and minerals in white tea provide these moisturizing benefits.

Anti-aging: White tea has anti-aging properties that can help slow down the aging process of hair, reducing dryness and brittleness. This is due to the high levels of antioxidants, such as EGCG and polyphenols, which protect the hair from environmental stressors.

UV protection: White tea provides UV protection for the hair, preventing dryness and damage caused by sun exposure. This is due to the presence of myricetin, a flavonoid that absorbs UV radiation, and other polyphenols that help filter out harmful rays.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and nutrients, white tea leaves help nourish and moisturize dry hair.
Buds: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, white tea buds can help promote hair growth and soothe a dry scalp.
Seeds: White tea seeds contain essential fatty acids and vitamins, which can help moisturize and protect dry hair from damage.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 tablespoons of dried organic white tea leaves and 1 cup of distilled water.
Bring the water to a boil, then remove it from heat and add the tea leaves.
Steep the tea for 5-7 minutes, then strain out the leaves using a fine mesh strainer.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature.
Pour the cooled tea over freshly washed hair, massaging it into the scalp and strands. Leave on for 10-15 minutes before rinsing thoroughly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with dry hair because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and nourish the scalp, promoting healthier hair growth. The tea is also rich in vitamins and minerals, such as calcium, magnesium, and potassium, which can help to strengthen and moisturize dry hair, reducing breakage and split ends. Additionally, German chamomile tea has been shown to help improve hair texture and shine, leaving it looking soft, silky, and more manageable. To use, simply brew a strong cup of tea, let it cool, and then use it as a rinse after shampooing. Leave it on for a few minutes before rinsing it out thoroughly. Regular use can help to improve the overall health and appearance of dry hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea can help reduce inflammation of the scalp, promoting hair growth and reducing dryness, due to the presence of active constituents like chamazulene and alpha-bisabolol.

Moisturizing: The emollient properties of German chamomile tea help to lock in moisture, reducing dryness and leaving hair soft and manageable, thanks to the rich content of flavonoids and essential oils.

Soothing: German chamomile tea has a calming effect on the scalp, reducing itchiness and irritation, which can lead to less dryness and breakage, because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds.
Strengthening: The tea can help to improve hair strength and elasticity, reducing breakage and split ends, due to the presence of flavonoids and other antioxidants that protect hair from damage.

Scalp circulation: German chamomile tea can help to improve blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing dryness, as a result of its essential oils and other active constituents that stimulate circulation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and essential oils, chamomile flowers help to nourish and moisturize dry hair.
Leaves: Containing flavonoids and other beneficial compounds, chamomile leaves can help to soothe and condition dry, damaged hair.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and antioxidants, chamomile seeds can help to strengthen and protect dry hair from further damage.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 tablespoons of dried German chamomile flowers.
Place the chamomile flowers in a heat-resistant container and pour 2 cups of boiling water over them.
Allow the chamomile flowers to steep in the water for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the chamomile tea, discarding the spent flowers and keeping the liquid.
Let the chamomile tea cool to a comfortable temperature before using it as a rinse on dry hair.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with dry hair because it is rich in silica and other minerals that can strengthen hair and improve its texture. Silica is known to promote hair growth and can help prevent hair breakage, which can be a common problem with dry hair. Additionally, horsetail tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a dry, itchy scalp, which can contribute to dry hair. Drinking horsetail tea or using it as a rinse can help hydrate and nourish dry hair, leaving it looking shinier and healthier. Regular use of horsetail tea can also help prevent further damage to dry hair, making it a great natural remedy for those looking to improve the condition of their hair.
remedy_properties: Rich in silica: Horsetail tea contains high levels of silica, a mineral that helps to strengthen hair and promote hair growth, making it an important property for dry hair which often lacks moisture and is prone to breakage.
Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce scalp inflammation and irritation, providing relief for dry, itchy scalps.
Antioxidant: Horsetail tea contains antioxidants that can help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry, brittle hair.

Promotes collagen production: Horsetail tea stimulates collagen production, which is important for maintaining the health of the hair and scalp, as collagen helps to keep the hair strong and flexible, reducing the likelihood of breakage and split ends in dry hair.
Diuretic: Horsetail tea has diuretic properties, which can help to increase blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and providing moisture to dry hair.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 tablespoons of dried horsetail herb.
Place the horsetail in a tea infuser or teapot with a built-in strainer.
Boil 2 cups of water and pour it over the horsetail, allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the horsetail from the water and let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature.
Pour the tea into a glass or mug and drink it, or use it as a rinse for dry hair after shampooing.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Horsetail tea made from rhyzomes is rich in silica, which helps to strengthen and moisturize dry hair.
Stems: The stems of horsetail tea plant contain high amounts of silica and other minerals that promote hair growth and prevent hair breakage.
Leaves: Horsetail leaves are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help to nourish and moisturize dry hair, promoting healthy hair growth.
Seeds: Horsetail seeds contain essential fatty acids and nutrients that help to strengthen and moisturize dry hair, promoting healthy hair growth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with dry hair because it is rich in nutrients and antioxidants that can improve the health of your hair. It contains flavonoids, which can help to strengthen hair follicles and promote hair growth. Fennel tea is also a natural anti-inflammatory, which can help to reduce scalp inflammation and dandruff, both of which can contribute to dry hair. Additionally, fennel tea is a natural emollient, which means that it can help to lock in moisture and keep your hair hydrated. This can help to reduce frizz and make your hair look shinier and healthier. Drinking fennel tea or using it as a rinse can provide your hair with the nutrients it needs to stay strong, moisturized, and looking its best.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of volatile oils like anethole, can help soothe an irritated scalp, promoting healthier hair growth and reducing dryness.
Hydrating: Fennel tea is a natural hydrator, containing significant amounts of moisture-retaining compounds, which can help keep the hair strands hydrated and reduce dryness.
Antioxidant: Fennel tea's antioxidant properties, derived from flavonoids and phenolic acids, can protect the hair from free radicals, reducing dryness, damage, and promoting hair health.
Emollient: Fennel tea has emollient properties, which help to soften and smooth the hair cuticle, reducing dryness and increasing shine.

Promotes Hair Growth: Fennel tea stimulates hair growth by improving blood circulation in the scalp, providing essential nutrients to the hair follicles, reducing dryness, and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in vitamins and minerals that help nourish the scalp and promote hair growth, making them an excellent choice for dry hair.
Leaves: Fennel leaves contain essential oils and antioxidants that can help moisturize and strengthen dry hair, promoting a healthy shine.
Flowers: Fennel flowers have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a dry, itchy scalp, providing relief and promoting hair growth.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of crushed fennel seeds; bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes to allow the water to absorb the herb's nutrients.
Strain the tea into a glass container, discarding the spent fennel seeds.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Before shampooing, pour the fennel tea over your dry hair, massaging it gently into your scalp, then let it sit for 15-20 minutes before rinsing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English ivy tea helps with dry hair because it contains rich nutrients and moisturizing properties that can nourish and hydrate the hair. The tea is known to be high in saponins, which can gently cleanse the scalp and remove build-up, while also promoting blood circulation. This increased blood flow can help to stimulate hair growth and reduce hair breakage. Additionally, the tea is packed with vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C and A, which can help to strengthen hair follicles and improve hair elasticity. By regularly using English ivy tea as a hair rinse or treatment, dry hair can become more moisturized, manageable, and healthy-looking.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: English ivy tea helps to moisturize dry hair by providing hydration and locking in moisture, thanks to the presence of mucilage, a type of polysaccharide.
Soothing inflammation: Its anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to saponins and flavonoids, help soothe an irritated scalp, promoting healthier hair growth.
Strengthening hair: English ivy tea contains high levels of silica and tannins, which strengthen hair strands, reducing breakage and split ends.
Encouraging hair growth: The tea stimulates blood circulation in the scalp, promoting hair growth and reducing hair loss, due to the presence of saponins and triterpenes.
Balancing sebum production: English ivy tea has astringent properties, which help regulate sebum production, preventing excess oiliness or dryness, thanks to the presence of tannins and saponins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nutrients and antioxidants, leaves help moisturize and nourish dry hair.
Stems: Contain flavonoids and saponins that can help strengthen and protect dry, damaged hair.
Rhizomes: Packed with vitamins and minerals, rhizomes promote hair growth and improve hair health in dry hair.
Buds: High in antioxidants and essential oils, buds help stimulate hair growth and soothe a dry scalp.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 English ivy leaves and rinse them thoroughly under running water.
Boil 2 cups of water in a pot, then add the cleaned ivy leaves and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
After simmering, strain the liquid to remove the leaves and let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Combine the cooled ivy tea with 1 cup of warm water and 1-2 tablespoons of your preferred carrier oil (e.g., olive, coconut, or jojoba oil).
Massage the herbal tea and oil mixture gently onto your scalp and damp hair, then cover your head with a shower cap or towel for 30 minutes before shampooing as usual.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dry hair with herbal teas is by rinsing your hair with cooled, steeped tea. Choose teas rich in nutrients, like chamomile, calendula, or rosemary. These herbs can help moisturize and strengthen dry hair. After shampooing, pour the cooled tea over your hair, massaging it into your scalp. Allow the tea to sit for a few minutes before rinsing with water. Do this regularly to see an improvement in your hair's texture and appearance.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry hair that are treated with herbal teas include dandruff, itchy scalp, and hair loss. Chamomile tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, making it an effective treatment for a dry, itchy scalp. Meanwhile, nettle tea can help stimulate hair growth and prevent hair loss due to its high mineral content. For dandruff, tea tree oil herbal tea can be beneficial as it has antifungal and antiseptic properties that can help alleviate the symptoms. However, it is important to note that while herbal teas can help treat these ailments, they should not replace medical treatment, especially for severe cases. Always consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
